report_name: AI-Driven Attacks Amplify Threats for MSPs and SMBs

threat_actors.name: AI-Equipped Threat Actors Targeting MSPs
threat_actors.description: A class of threat actors described in the Guardz 2026 report who leverage Artificial Intelligence to accelerate attacks against Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and their SMB clients, primarily through the abuse of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools.
threat_actors.goals: Compromise MSPs to gain downstream access to their SMB clients for financial gain through Business Email Compromise (BEC) or to establish long-term persistent access.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors use AI to craft highly convincing, personalized phishing emails at scale, leading to credential theft and Business Email Compromise (BEC).

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abuse legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools such as ScreenConnect, AteraAgent, and MeshAgent to gain unauthorized, persistent access to client networks, evading detection by using trusted software.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Once an MSP is breached, often through phishing, attackers use the compromised MSP accounts to gain legitimate access to the RMM platform.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The compromise of a single Managed Service Provider (MSP) is used as a vector to attack all of their downstream Small and Medium-sized Business (SMB) clients, representing a severe supply chain attack.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Attackers execute malicious PowerShell scripts, often encoded, via compromised RMM agents to perform actions on managed endpoints.

tools.name: ScreenConnect
tools.description: A legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tool abused by attackers to gain unauthorized, persistent access to the networks of MSP clients.

tools.name: AteraAgent
tools.description: A legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) agent observed being abused by attackers for unauthorized access to client networks.

tools.name: MeshAgent
tools.description: A legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) agent observed being abused by attackers for unauthorized access to client networks.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mentioned as an example of an unusual process that an RMM agent should not be spawning, indicating its potential use for credential dumping post-compromise.

identities.name: Managed Service Providers
identities.description: A primary target for supply chain attacks, where their compromise leads to breaches of their downstream SMB clients.

identities.name: Small and Medium-sized Businesses
identities.description: The ultimate victims of the supply chain attacks, with the report stating that nine out of ten have compromised users.

indicators.name: Suspicious Process Spawns from RMM Tools
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Process Spawns from RMM Tools
id: 2b9e1c3d-8f4a-4b6e-8c7d-9a1b3e5f7c9a
status: experimental
description: Detects when a legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tool spawns a suspicious process like a shell or credential dumper, which may indicate RMM abuse by an attacker.
references:
  - https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/guardz-report-9-out-of-10-smbs-have-compromised-users-as-ai-driven-attacks-reshape-the-msp-threat-landscape-302128795.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-28
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe'
      - '\AteraAgent.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mimikatz.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
indicators.description: Detects when a legitimate RMM agent process (e.g., ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe) spawns suspicious child processes like PowerShell or Mimikatz, as suggested in the report's hunting hints.

indicators.name: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell Command
id: 5d8f2a1b-9e3c-4a6d-8b1e-7c9a3b5f7e9d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of encoded commands in PowerShell, a common technique for obfuscation used by attackers.
references:
  - https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/guardz-report-9-out-of-10-smbs-have-compromised-users-as-ai-driven-attacks-reshape-the-msp-threat-landscape-302128795.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-28
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of encoded PowerShell commands, a hunting hint provided in the article for identifying malicious script execution via RMM tools.

indicators.name: Known RMM Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Known RMM Tool Execution
id: 8c1d4e6f-7b9a-4d3c-8a1b-9e3c5f7d9a1b
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of known Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools. While often legitimate, their execution should be monitored as they are a primary vector for MSP supply chain attacks.
references:
  - https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/guardz-report-9-out-of-10-smbs-have-compromised-users-as-ai-driven-attacks-reshape-the-msp-threat-landscape-302128795.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-28
tags:
  - attack.t1219
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe'
      - '\AteraAgent.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of RMM tools mentioned in the report. While these are legitimate tools, monitoring their execution is critical as they are a key vector in the described attacks.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Process Spawns from RMM Tools
